LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LONHYN JASINSKYJ NTC OF HEARING ON DF'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT LONHYN JASINSKYJ'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE PENDING ACTION INVOLVES MULTIPLE PARTIES AND IF THE COURT WERE TO GRANT THIS PETITION, THERE IS A POSSIBILITY OF CONFLICTING RULES ON COMMON ISSUES OF LAW AND FACT. CC 1281.2; CRONUS INVESTMENTS, INC. V. CONCIERGE SERVICES (2005) 35 CAL. 4TH 376, 393. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO RESPOND TO COMPLAINT IN 14 DAYS IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)

Ntc Of Hearing On Df'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sandra J. McNabb, Esq. of MCGLASHAN & SARRAIL 650-341-2585, appearing for defendant / moving party Lonhyn Jasinsky
Glen M. Belovsky, Esq. (415-241-2880), specially appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Maria Plascencia

Ruling:
DEFENDANT LONHYN JASINSKYJ Ntc Of Hearing On Df'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LONHYN JASINSKYJ'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is denied. The pending action involves multiple parties and if the Court were to grant this petition, there is a possibility of conflicting rules on common issues of law and fact. CC 1281.2; Cronus Investments, Inc. v. Concierge Services (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 376, 393. Defendant's request to respond to complaint in 14 days is granted. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM)
